note: Wisconsin is the filing state but no separate Wisconsin addendum was found in Exhibit J-1. Michigan addendum appears in both front matter (pp. 5-6) and Exhibit J-1 (pp. 257-258).

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under Cal. Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor's liquidated damages formula (up to 60x monthly royalty) may not be collectible in California

overrides.override_summary: Cal. Business and Professions Code Sections 20000-20043 provide termination/renewal rights that supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee has statutory protections against arbitrary termination or non-renewal beyond FA terms

overrides.override_summary: General release may not waive California franchise law claims (Corp. Code Section 31512, B&P Code Section 20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: Pre-signing and transfer/renewal releases cannot extinguish California franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: New York choice-of-law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may govern the franchise relationship for California franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Virginia venue requirement may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisee may be able to litigate in California rather than Virginia

overrides.override_summary: Material modifications require prior delivery of updated FDD (Corp. Code Section 31125)
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor must provide updated disclosure before requesting material FA changes

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from liability imposed by Hawaii franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii franchise law claims survive any release

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs Illinois licensees; venue/choice-of-law provisions will not waive IFDA rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law supersedes NY choice-of-law and VA venue for Illinois franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of compliance with IFDA or any Illinois law is void (Section 41), though settlement agreements and arbitration are permitted
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot contractually waive IFDA protections

overrides.override_summary: Maryland franchise law may supersede FA regarding termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland provides statutory termination protections beyond FA terms

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law; 3-year statute of limitations from grant
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees can litigate locally despite VA venue clause

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from Maryland franchise law liability; no waiver of franchise law claims permitted
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchise law claims survive releases; no waiver, questionnaire, or acknowledgment can waive claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; 30-day cure opportunity required
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan voids FA provisions allowing termination without good cause or without adequate cure opportunity

overrides.override_summary: Must fairly compensate franchisee on non-renewal (if term <5 years and noncompete applies or <6 months notice)
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisees from unfair non-renewal terms (though Canopy's 23-year term likely exceeds 5-year threshold)

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require arbitration or litigation outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia venue clause is void for Michigan franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release that deprives franchisee of Michigan Franchise Investment Law protections
overrides.why_it_matters: Pre-signing and transfer releases cannot waive Michigan franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for non-renewal required
overrides.why_it_matters: Substantially longer cure/notice periods than FA's 10-30 days

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages provisions prohibited (Minnesota Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Entire liquidated damages formula unenforceable in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota; cannot require jury trial waiver
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia venue and jury waiver clauses unenforceable in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Release language shall not relieve franchisor from Minnesota Franchise Investment Law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchise law claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: New York Franchises Law requires NY law govern causes of action under NY Franchises Law; NY GBL Article 33 may supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law governs despite general NY choice-of-law clause

overrides.override_summary: General release may not be enforceable under New York law for renewal/transfer
overrides.why_it_matters: Transfer and renewal releases may be challenged

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate upon any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law may provide franchisee with statutory termination rights not in FA

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor cannot collect any liquidated damages from North Dakota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law supersedes NY law; FA governed by North Dakota law
overrides.why_it_matters: Complete override of NY choice-of-law

overrides.override_summary: All venue/jurisdiction provisions outside North Dakota deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia venue clause completely voided

overrides.override_summary: Release shall not relieve franchisor from North Dakota Franchise Disclosure Act liability
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims survive all releases

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Right to jury trial preserved in North Dakota

overrides.override_summary: Release signed as condition of transfer/renewal will not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims survive transfer/renewal releases

overrides.override_summary: Venue/jurisdiction outside RI or application of non-RI law void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate locally under RI franchise law

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act Section 13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: Any FA termination ground that doesn't constitute 'reasonable cause' may be unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA termination and renewal provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchise law provides additional termination protections

overrides.override_summary: Release will not include WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Broad releases void unless negotiated post-execution with separate lawyers

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in event of conflict of laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington law overrides NY choice-of-law for franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location; litigation for franchise sales or FIPA claims may be in Washington
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia venue clause overridden for Washington franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void against employees earning <$100K/year and contractors earning <$250K/year (RCW 49.62.020/030); no-hire restrictions on franchisee employees prohibited (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits enforceability of any noncompete provisions and prohibits no-poach restrictions
